summary: Matched a cluster of gm_1601 and gmmain_lib functions by replacing placeholder stubs and weak M2C/UNK signatures with typed implementations, struct field accesses, bool-return helpers, mask manipulation, and persistent fighter data cleanup. The PR also refined game-mode data structs and headers so gm_1601, gm_16AE, gmmain_lib, and one fighter caller use consistent boolean and typed pointer interfaces.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_1601 functions around addresses 80163D24 through 801641E4, including boolean scans over 0x19 persistent fighter entries, KO/stat total accumulation helpers, item mask queries, item mask remapping, and stage mask toggling.

Implemented gm_801693BC as a bool function checking special player conditions and character IDs against CKIND_BOY through CKIND_GIRL, with the comment identifying the behavior as detecting whether a player is Fighting Wireframe.

Implemented gm_801694A0, gm_8016A164, and gm_8016A1F8, covering active/player-state counting, single-player/match-state reset, slot type reset for players 1 through 5, and a boolean accessor for lbl_8046B488.unk_10_b1.
Replaced M2C_FIELD accesses with named struct fields, including MatchEnd.n_winners, lbl_8046B488.x7, and lbl_8046B488.xF.
Changed fn_80169364 from UNK_T to returning struct lbl_8046B488_t* and exposed the forward declaration in gm_1601.h while removing a redundant static declaration from gm_16AE.static.h.
Expanded struct lbl_8046B488_t in gm_1601.static.h to name byte fields at offsets 0x7, 0x8, and 0xF before the existing bitfields at offset 0x10.
Added the static item remap table lbl_803B7844 used by fn_801640B0 to translate 32 item bits, with 0x23 treated as a sentinel skip value.

Restructured struct gmm_x1F2C in gm/types.h so the data from offset 0x7C onward is represented as a nested x7C struct containing bitfields and the former x7E through xA8 fields; this drove corresponding access changes in gmmain_lib.c.

Updated gmmain_lib functions to access persistent fighter fields through base[arg0].x7C.*, changed several return types to bool, and changed KO/stat pointer accessors gmMainLib_8015D74C and gmMainLib_8015D7A4 to return u16*.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015EEC8 to zero each fighter's x7C persistent data for 0x19 entries and then clear a 0xD8-byte range starting at thing.x1A68.
Simplified boolean call sites in ft_0D14.c and gm_16AE.c by using direct truthiness or logical negation instead of temporary int comparisons.
smart_moves: Used struct refinements to eliminate M2C_FIELD and raw offset accesses, making matched code cleaner and creating reusable type knowledge for later gm_1601 work.

Changed signatures from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and s32-return booleans to bool/u8/u16 pointer types where the implementation showed binary flag semantics, reducing casts and improving call-site readability.
Nested the 0x7C persistent fighter data block inside gmm_x1F2C rather than leaving many flat fields, allowing memzero(&data->x7C, sizeof(data->x7C)) to match a whole substructure cleanup pattern.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack padding with PAD_STACK in functions such as gm_801693BC, gm_801694A0, gm_8016A164, and gmMainLib_8015EEC8.

Used simple bounded loops with explicit constants already visible in surrounding code, especially 0x19 for persistent fighter slots and 0x20 for item mask bits, which likely helped match loop codegen.
Modeled item mask conversion with a static remap table and sentinel check instead of open-coded switch logic, matching the data-driven shape implied by lbl_803B7844.
decomp_lessons: When a group of accessor functions all index the same persistent data base, matching one set of offsets can reveal a nested substructure; update the struct first and then propagate field accesses through all accessors.
Boolean helper functions should usually be typed as bool once callers use them only as predicates; this can remove temporary variables and produce cleaner matching conditionals.

Replacing M2C_FIELD with named fields is often coupled to header/static-header cleanup: add forward declarations or concrete struct fields only where needed, and remove stale local extern/static declarations.
For reset functions, look for memzero over a substructure instead of individual field clears; grouping fields into a named nested struct can make the source match the binary memory-clear pattern.
Mask manipulation code can require 64-bit constants such as 1LL << item for item masks, while stage masks may use 32-bit shifts; preserve the width used by the backing field.
Use evidence from nearby accessors to choose signedness: this PR changed KO/stat halfword accessors from s16* to u16* and matching gm_1601 wrappers return u16.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names such as gm_80163DE8, gm_8016403C, gm_801693BC, and gmMainLib_8015EEC8 where semantic names were not available.
Used fn_ prefix for local/unknown-purpose helpers such as fn_80163D24, fn_80163D74, fn_80163FA4, fn_801640B0, and fn_80169364.
Used x-offset field names for partially understood globals and structs, including lbl_8046B488.x7, x8, xF, and gmm_x1F2C.x7C.xA0.
Added a semantic comment for gm_801693BC: "Player is Fighting Wireframe"; this is the clearest behavior label present in the diff.
Named mask parameters by domain where clear from use, such as item, item_mask, stage, and enable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK to preserve stack frame shape in newly matched functions.
Converted `if (func() == 0)` to `if (!func())` after bool return typing, as seen with gm_8016A1F8 in gm_16AE.c.
Removed a temporary return variable in ftCo_800D3680 and tested gm_801693BC directly, likely relying on the new bool signature for matching and readability.
Used `ckind -= CKIND_BOY; if (ckind <= CKIND_GIRL - CKIND_BOY)` range-check form in gm_801693BC, a common decomp tactic for matching compiler output for contiguous enum ranges.
Used assignment inside a conditional in fn_801640B0, `if ((shift = lbl_803B7844[(u8) i]) != 0x23)`, preserving a compact table-lookup-and-branch structure.
Used `memzero(&data->x7C, sizeof(data->x7C))` and `memzero(&gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x1A68, 0xD8)` to match block clears instead of hand-written field resets.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
There was one issue comment counted in the PR metadata, but the excerpt contains no human comment body, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_8046B488_t fields x7, x8, and xF to semantic names.
Find later PRs touching gmm_x1F2C.x7C to validate the nested persistent fighter data layout.
Search for gm_801693BC callers to confirm all call sites treat it as the Fighting Wireframe predicate.
Search for lbl_803B7844 or fn_801640B0 to determine the semantic item order represented by the item mask remap table.
Check later gmmain_lib matching work around gmMainLib_8015EF30 and the cleanup/reset functions following gmMainLib_8015EEC8.
